_comment: Autonomous post+repost decision-maker (src/triggers/ai_manager.py). Each Claude call now produces a BATCH of up to posts_per_batch posts instead of just one -- only the first is fired immediately, the rest are queued in state and drained one per subsequent run (natural hourly-cron spacing), so total posts/day (target ~10-14) can be much higher than the Claude call cadence itself (~6-7 calls/day, kept low deliberately to stay well within config/claude_budget.json's cap even on Sonnet 5 at full post-intro pricing). Since a call costs money regardless of outcome, the prompt pushes Claude to find at least one genuinely worth-it post per call rather than defaulting to 'no action' -- combined with posts_per_batch raised to 3, this squeezes more real output out of the same (few) calls. No images and no links, by deliberate choice -- second_part_every_n_posts nudges Claude's own per-post second_part decision (a genuine continuation posted as a reply, roughly 1 in second_part_every_n posts) instead; the account's own profile is meant to be enough to inform a reader with no outbound clicks needed. Reposts are deliberately kept sparse (max_reposts_per_day: 3, max_reposts_per_call: 1) so the profile reads mostly as our own original posts rather than a mixed feed.
